name: PASTIS Neuropsychological Outcomes Trial Extract
population_or_context: Older adults with symptomatic cerebral small vessel disease; indirect for mostly healthy men age 30 to 50.
sample_size: 55 completed participants; mean age 66.8 years, range 52-87; 15 female and 40 male.
methods: Double-blind randomized placebo-controlled crossover trial secondary outcome analysis. Participants received tadalafil 20 mg and placebo at two visits at least 7 days apart, with cognitive testing before and 3-5 hours after dosing.
population_profile: Older people with symptomatic cerebral small vessel disease, median MoCA 26; not a healthy-adult cognitive-enhancement population and not directly representative of mostly healthy men age 30 to 50.
tadalafil_exposure: Single oral tadalafil 20 mg dose, compared with placebo in crossover order.
indication_context: Experimental off-label vascular cognitive impairment/small vessel disease research context.
study_design: Phase 2 double-blind randomized placebo-controlled crossover trial secondary neuropsychological outcome analysis.
comparator_context: Within-participant placebo comparison across two visits separated by at least 7 days.
safety_findings: The paper reports no serious adverse reactions to the trial intervention; the study was not designed as a comprehensive safety review for off-label use in healthy adults.
directness_to_target_population: Indirect: tadalafil-specific and human, but older symptomatic SVD population with acute dosing, not healthy men age 30 to 50 and not chronic low-dose use.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Secondary cognitive endpoints, small sample, brief single-dose exposure, limited neuropsychological battery, and indirect disease population limit inference for healthy-adult cognition.
